welcome.title: Welcome to Wildfire Simulation Platform!
welcome.description: Let's take a quick tour to help you get started with the Wildfire Simulation Platform.
welcome.tip: This tour will only show once. You can skip it anytime or restart it from the sidebar.

newAssessment.title: Create New Simulation
newAssessment.description: Ready to get started? Click this button to create your first wildfire simulation. This will open the area selection wizard.
newAssessment.tip: When you click this, you'll enter a dedicated tour for the area selection process!

profile.title: Profile & Settings
profile.description: Access your profile, account settings, and user preferences. Manage your account and access additional options.

navigation.title: Dashboard Navigation
navigation.description: Access your main dashboard to view your projects, recent assessments, and overall statistics. This is your home base.

simulations.title: Wildfire Simulations
simulations.description: Create and manage your wildfire simulation models. Start new simulations, configure parameters, and review existing runs.

map.title: Interactive Map
map.description: Your main workspace for geographical analysis. View map layers, select areas for simulation, and visualize wildfire risk data.

locations.title: Saved Locations
locations.description: Manage your saved locations and areas of interest. Quick access to frequently analyzed regions.

reports.title: Simulation Reports
reports.description: View detailed simulation reports, export data, and analyze historical trends and patterns.

technologies.title: Simulation Parameters
technologies.description: Configure wildfire simulation parameters such as fuel models, weather, and ignition sources.
technologies.tip: Parameters can be customized per scenario for accurate wildfire simulations.

layers.title: Map Layers
layers.description: Control map layers and overlays. Choose base maps, toggle data layers, and customize your map view.

documentation.title: Documentation
documentation.description: Open the platform docs for detailed guides, API references, and best practices.
documentation.tip: Opens in a new tab so you keep your current workflow.

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.description: Share your thoughts and suggestions with us. Your feedback helps improve the platform.

complete.title: You're All Set!
complete.description: You've completed the tour! You're now ready to start creating wildfire simulations.
complete.nextStepsDescription: Try creating your first wildfire simulation or explore the interactive map.
complete.restartTourDescription: You can restart this tour anytime from the sidebar.

welcome.title: Wildfire Simulation Guide
welcome.description: Welcome! This tour will guide you through creating a wildfire simulation for your chosen region.
welcome.tip: You'll learn how to configure your model, draw areas, and create your simulation.

modelName.title: Model Name
modelName.description: Give your wildfire simulation model a descriptive name. This helps you identify it later in your dashboard.
modelName.tip: Good names: "Galicia Spain 2024" or "Galician Forest Scenario"

dateRange.title: Simulation Period
dateRange.description: Choose the time period for your wildfire analysis.
dateRange.tip: Longer periods provide more comprehensive wildfire analysis

resolution.title: Time Resolution
resolution.description: Set the time interval for calculations. Higher resolution provides more detailed results but takes longer to process.
resolution.tip: 60 minutes is recommended for most simulations

technologies.title: Parameters
technologies.description: Configure wildfire simulation parameters such as fuel models, weather, and ignition sources, and fine-tune advanced settings.
technologies.tip: Click to view and customize settings for your simulation

mapContainer.title: Interactive Map
mapContainer.description: This is your main workspace. Draw polygons on the map to select areas for your wildfire simulation.
mapContainer.tip: You can zoom and pan the map to explore different regions

search.title: Location Search
search.description: Use this search box to quickly find specific locations by name or address.
search.tip: Try typing a location name to see instant results

save.title: Create Simulation
save.description: Once you've configured everything, click here to create your wildfire simulation model and start the analysis.
save.tip: All fields must be completed before you can save

calculationStatus.title: Calculation status
calculationStatus.description: This line tells you whether static dates are loading, unavailable, errored, or ready for selection.
calculationStatus.tip: If this shows a blocker, the continue button stays disabled until the date selection is valid.

calculationMode.title: Calculation mode
calculationMode.description: Choose static mode for one available fire-risk date or dynamic mode for a date range that the backend can process later.
calculationMode.tip: The status text below the date control tells you whether available static dates are loaded and whether the selected date is valid.

areaInput.title: Area input
areaInput.description: Draw the area directly on the map or upload a GeoJSON Polygon/MultiPolygon boundary.
areaInput.tip: The model can continue only after one valid area of interest is available.

areaStatus.title: AOI status
areaStatus.description: This status confirms whether the boundary is missing, drawn, uploaded, or blocked by an upload error.

optionalLayers.title: Risk signals
optionalLayers.description: These toggles control which optional signals are sent with the model request, including fire-weather, terrain, and historical fires.
optionalLayers.tip: Keep fire-weather enabled for normal risk runs; disabling it is useful only for a baseline comparison.

finalReview.title: Validation status
finalReview.description: The review step checks the model name, date, AOI, risk components, and buffer before the run can start.

saveSummary.title: Save summary
saveSummary.description: This final summary shows the values that will be saved with the model and sent to the calculation workflow.

feedback.title: Send Feedback
feedback.subtitle: Help us improve your experience
feedback.subject: Subject
feedback.subjectPlaceholder: e.g., Map loading issue, Feature suggestion...
feedback.messagePlaceholder: Please describe your feedback, suggestions, or issues in detail...
feedback.imageDropzone: Click to upload or drag and drop
feedback.ratingDescription: How would you rate your experience?
feedback.gdprNotice: Your feedback is processed in compliance with GDPR regulations.

success.title: Feedback Submitted!
success.message: Thank you for your feedback. We'll review it and get back to you if needed.

errors.loginRequired: Please log in to submit feedback
errors.submitFailed: Failed to submit feedback. Please try again later.
errors.invalidImageType: Please select a valid image file (PNG, JPG, GIF)
errors.imageTooLarge: Image size must be less than 15MB
errors.payloadTooLarge: The attachment is too large. Please use smaller images.

myFeedback.title: My Feedback

weather.temperatureTooltip: Current temperature in Celsius and Fahrenheit

simulationComparison.title: Simulation Comparison
simulationComparison.subtitle: Compare results across multiple simulation runs
simulationComparison.selectModelsDescription: Choose two completed simulation models above to analyze their differences in energy generation, costs, and key performance indicators.
simulationComparison.startBySelectingBaseline: Start by selecting a baseline model
simulationComparison.summarySub: Key changes between the baseline and comparison model.
simulationComparison.dominantClassSub: Largest share of valid pixels: {{baseline}}% in baseline, {{comparison}}% in comparison.
simulationComparison.analyzedAreaSub: Area is calculated from valid non-nodata pixels, so it can differ between model outputs.
simulationComparison.badDeltaText: means the comparison model is more severe.
simulationComparison.goodDeltaText: usually means risk shifted toward safer classes.
simulationComparison.distributionSub: Share of pixels per risk level. Lighter bar = baseline, solid = comparison.
simulationComparison.insightChartsSub: These maps summarize the sampled raster cells from the comparison model. Use them to see risk concentration and exact class areas.
simulationComparison.noMapSamples: Geographic map samples are not available yet.
simulationComparison.sampleMapNote: This is a sampled raster view, not the street map. The filled cells show the same risk classes used by the Fire Risk layer.
simulationComparison.geoHeatmapSub: Smooth view of where risk is concentrated. Red means the strongest sampled risk.
simulationComparison.choroplethMapSub: Exact sampled cells by risk class. Use this when the smooth heatmap hides cell boundaries.
simulationComparison.noConfiguredLayer: No GeoServer layer is configured for this model yet.
simulationComparison.loadLayerFailed: Failed to load layer from GeoServer
simulationComparison.mapHint: Pan/zoom either map — views stay in sync.
simulationComparison.mapHintIndependent: These models cover different areas — each map shows its own model.

privacy.sharedWithCarto: By accepting, the following details will be shared with CARTO and OSMF respectively:

privacy.reviewPolicies: For more information, please review the respective privacy policies:

welcome.description: Let's set up your preferences to get started

welcome.step2: Set your weather and map preferences

errors.layerLoad: Failed to load layer from GeoServer
errors.tileLoad: Some tiles failed to load from GeoServer ({{count}})

empty.title: No result for this model yet.
empty.hint: The simulation output will appear here once processing finishes.

risk.notReady: Metrics will be available once the layer is configured in GeoServer.

hero.title: Wildfire risk intelligence for rural communities
hero.subtitle: A geospatial platform for municipalities, civil protection and researchers to map, model and act on wildfire risk — turning local knowledge and data into proactive protection before the flames ignite.

challenge.title: Wildfires are no longer isolated disasters — they are a seasonal crisis
challenge.p1: Across Southern Europe, rising temperatures, longer droughts and unpredictable weather are turning forests into fuel. Rural depopulation means less land management and greater vegetation build-up, making outbreaks more intense and harder to control.
challenge.p2: Recent seasons in Portugal, Spain and Greece were among the worst on record — costing lives, destroying ecosystems and displacing communities, while threatening agriculture, forestry, tourism and basic safety. Local actors remain under-equipped and under-supported.

hotter.title: Hotter, drier seasons
hotter.text: Climate shifts extend the fire season year on year.

vegetation.title: Vegetation build-up
vegetation.text: Depopulation leaves landscapes unmanaged and fuel-rich.

cascading.title: Cascading impact
cascading.text: Ecosystems, economies and rural life are all at risk.

tooling.title: Limited tooling
tooling.text: Communities lack shared, data-driven decision support.

challenge.imgAlt1: Wildfire spreading along a rural road

platform.title: From an area on the map to shared risk evidence
platform.subtitle: The platform combines 3D building and terrain data, geospatial analysis and a wildfire-risk engine into one role-appropriate workflow.
platform.imgAlt: Wildfire response crew in the field

define.title: Define an area
define.text: Search a municipality and draw the area of interest directly on the interactive map.

run.title: Run a risk model
run.text: Queue a wildfire-risk calculation over your AOI with the configured engine.

inspect.title: Inspect the results
inspect.text: Explore the classified risk overlay with optional fuel-model and vegetation layers.

compare.title: Compare & share
compare.text: Compare scenarios side by side, export artefacts and share with stakeholders.

capabilities.title: Everything you need to assess and explain risk

mapping.title: Interactive mapping
mapping.text: 2D maps with polygon drawing, place search and persistent operating areas.

engine.title: Wildfire-risk engine
engine.text: Classified risk rasters with calibrated severity and high-risk area estimates.

metrics.title: Metrics & comparison
metrics.text: Risk cards, distribution charts, deltas and trends across model runs.

fuel.title: Fuel & vegetation layers
fuel.text: Optional FMT and NDVI overlays to understand why the risk map behaves as it does.

multilingual.title: Multilingual & accessible
multilingual.text: Available in 8 languages with clear labels and guided tours for every role.

secure.title: Secure & governed
secure.text: Role-based access, audited results and EU-compliant privacy controls.

planners.title: Municipal & civil-protection planners
planners.text: Create and explain wildfire-risk assessments and brief decision-makers.

analysts.title: Forest-fire risk analysts
analysts.text: Validate GIS layers, risk metrics and engine outputs at expert level.

researchers.title: Researchers & GIS teams
researchers.text: Download GeoTIFF/ZIP outputs and study fuel models and vegetation indices.

leads.title: Workshop leads & reviewers
leads.text: Run training sessions and review shared risk evidence with limited access.

contact.title: Start a conversation
contact.subtitle: Tell us about your region and how you'd like to use the platform. The team will get back to you — no account required to reach out.
contact.responseTime: We typically respond within a few working days.
contact.name: Name
contact.subject: Subject
contact.successText: Thanks for reaching out — we'll be in touch soon.
contact.validationError: Please complete name, email, subject and message.
contact.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.
contact.agree: By submitting you agree to our
